title: Network Automation Lab 005: RESTCONF
description: Submission template for the existing RESTCONF lab covering REST API queries, HTTPS safety, read-only validation, and professional API evidence.

required_evidence.label: RESTCONF target
required_evidence.description: Identify the device, API endpoint category, or lab target. Do not submit credentials or tokens.

required_evidence.label: API query goal
required_evidence.description: Explain what the RESTCONF query is intended to retrieve or validate.

required_evidence.label: Safe request details
required_evidence.description: Document method, endpoint path category, headers used in general terms, or script name. Do not include credentials, bearer tokens, cookies, or private data.

required_evidence.label: Response observations
required_evidence.description: Summarize the response, status code, returned fields, or useful observations without leaking sensitive values.

required_evidence.label: Validation
required_evidence.description: Explain how you proved the RESTCONF result was accurate or useful.

required_evidence.label: Security note
required_evidence.description: Explain how HTTPS, credential handling, scope, and read-only API behavior were handled safely.

required_evidence.label: Professional summary
required_evidence.description: Write a short network automation API summary.

completion_signals: student explains the API query goal
safety_rules: Do not submit credentials, API tokens, bearer tokens, cookies, private keys, or secrets.
Prefer read-only RESTCONF queries for beginner labs.
Do not change device configuration through RESTCONF unless the lab explicitly requires it.
